concerns.text: FR-002 mandates execution of three distinct tasks (summarization, bug detection, function completion). Task T018 implements a fallback to 'Reconstruction-Only' mode if annotations are missing, effectively allowing the project to drop 'summarization' and 'bug detection' without a corresponding task to explicitly implement the full three-task logic or a task to formally reduce scope in the spec. The FR requires the system to execute all three; the tasks only guarantee one if data is missing.

SC-003 requires dataset coverage of N ≥ 5,000 valid functions. Task T015 explicitly caps the count at N=1,000 to meet Plan constraints. There is no task to update SC-003 in the spec or to document the statistical power justification for the reduction, leaving the Success Criterion unmet by the implementation plan.

FR-005 requires identifying thresholds where accuracy drops below '[deferred] of the dataset's mean accuracy' with a sensitivity sweep. Task T025b hardcodes '70% of mean accuracy' as the threshold. There is no task to implement the dynamic calculation of the '[deferred]' baseline or to sweep the threshold relative to that dynamic value, creating a coverage gap for the specific FR requirement.

SC-005 requires measuring the multiple-comparison error rate against a correction method. Task T027 describes applying the correction (Bonferroni/FDR) but lacks a specific task to calculate and report the measured error rate or verify the correction's success as a distinct output, leaving the 'measured against' part of the SC unimplemented.
